answers.value: B.S. in Business Administration, concentration in Human Resources Management
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value:
• SHRM-CP (Society for Human Resource Management – Certified Professional), obtained 2024-06. Completed a 12-week intensive prep course through the Sacramento HR Association, focusing on employment law, talent acquisition, employee engagement, and compensation/benefits.

• Certificate in HR Analytics (online program through Coursera in partnership with the University of Pennsylvania), completed 2023-11. Coursework included data visualization in Excel, basic SQL queries, and using HR metrics (turnover, time-to-fill, cost-per-hire) to support business decisions.

• Completed a 2-day workshop on California Wage & Hour Law and Leave Management (FMLA/CFRA/PDL) hosted by the Northern California Human Resources Association in 2023. Took extensive notes and created internal reference guides for my current team.

• Annual harassment prevention training (California-compliant, AB 1825/2053) from 2021–2025, including bystander intervention and respectful workplace modules.

• Internal training on HRIS systems, including ADP Workforce Now and UKG Pro. Helped configure workflows for onboarding and time-off approvals.

• Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) micro-certification from HR University, completed 2022-08, with a capstone project where I designed an inclusive recruiting checklist and a structured interview guide to reduce bias.

• CPR/First Aid certified through the American Red Cross (current through 2026-09) from volunteering at community events and wellness fairs organized by my previous employer.

answers.field_label: Are You Presently Enrolled In School
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value:
HR & PEOPLE OPERATIONS EXPERIENCE:
Over the past four years, I’ve worked in HR support roles for two mid-sized companies (a logistics firm and a healthcare services provider), with day-to-day responsibilities that closely mirror those of an HR Generalist. I have hands-on experience with:

• Onboarding & New Hire Processing:
  - Prepared and sent offer letters, background check authorizations, and new-hire packets.
  - Collected and verified I-9 documentation and completed E-Verify within required timelines.
  - Entered new hires into ADP Workforce Now and later UKG Pro, ensuring accurate job codes, locations, and benefit eligibility dates.
  - Conducted first-day orientations, walking employees through policies, payroll schedule, benefits overview, and safety procedures.

• Employee Relations & Policy Communication:
  - Served as a first point of contact for employees with questions about PTO, leaves, benefits enrollment, and payroll discrepancies.
  - Helped draft and update FAQ documents and internal memos when policies changed, e.g., remote work guidelines during COVID-19 and subsequent returns to office.
  - Assisted HR Manager with documenting minor attendance and performance issues by compiling email trails, timekeeping records, and preparing summary notes.

• Recruiting & Talent Acquisition Support:
  - Posted job openings on Indeed, LinkedIn, and company careers pages using structured job descriptions.
  - Reviewed resumes and screened candidates for entry-level and mid-level roles (warehouse associates, customer service reps, medical billing specialists).
  - Conducted 20–30 minute phone screens using standardized questions, then compiled notes and recommendations for hiring managers.
  - Coordinated interview schedules, sent calendar invites, and followed up with candidates on next steps or declinations.

• HRIS, DATA & COMPLIANCE:
  - Regularly ran reports from ADP/UKG on headcount, turnover, and time-off balances for use in monthly leadership meetings.
  - Helped audit personnel files to ensure required documents (I-9, signed handbook acknowledgments, emergency contacts) were present and filed correctly.
  - Monitored upcoming work authorization expiration dates and coordinated with affected employees and managers.
  - Assisted with annual EEO-1 data collection by confirming job categories, locations, and demographics from HRIS and paper files.

TECHNICAL SKILLS:
• HRIS: ADP Workforce Now, UKG Pro, and some experience with Paylocity.
• Applicant Tracking: BreezyHR and basic use of Greenhouse (scheduling, feedback forms).
• Productivity Tools: Advanced Excel (VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, pivot tables, conditional formatting), Word (policies, letters), PowerPoint (training decks), and Outlook/Teams for communication.
• File Management: Comfortable maintaining both digital and physical personnel files in alignment with retention and confidentiality requirements.

COMMUNICATION & INTERPERSONAL SKILLS:
• Able to explain complex policies (e.g., FMLA/CFRA leave, eligibility waiting periods for benefits) in plain language, both in writing and verbally.
• Experienced in de-escalating emotional conversations by listening first, acknowledging concerns, and then walking through options and company policies.
• Comfortable presenting to small groups; for example, I regularly led new-hire orientation groups of 5–10 employees and facilitated Q&A sessions.

MILITARY SERVICE:
• No military service.

LANGUAGES:
• English (native)
• Vietnamese (conversational; can assist employees and family members with basic questions and translations of simple documents).

ACCOMPLISHMENTS & PROJECTS:
• Turnover Analysis Project (2023):
  - Pulled 18 months of termination data from ADP, categorized reasons (voluntary vs. involuntary), and analyzed trends by department and shift.
  - Identified that voluntary turnover was particularly high for night shift warehouse staff within their first six months.
  - Presented findings to HR Manager, who used the analysis to support implementing a shift-differential increase and a revised onboarding program for that group.

• Handbook Update Support (2022–2024):
  - Assisted in revising employee handbook sections on attendance, remote work, and anti-harassment policies.
  - Compared our policies with template language from SHRM and ensured alignment with California requirements, especially around meal and rest breaks.

• Onboarding Process Improvement:
  - Noticed that many new hires were missing certain forms on day one, causing delays.
  - Proposed an online pre-boarding checklist with clear instructions and deadlines.
  - After implementing this checklist, incomplete paperwork incidents dropped noticeably (informally from 1 in 3 to around 1 in 10 new hires).

Overall, my skills and experience are centered on being highly organized, detail-oriented, and people-focused, with a strong desire to continue growing into a more independent HR Generalist role where I can own processes from start to finish.

answers.field_label: Special Requests Or Needs For Work Schedule
answers.value:
I strongly prefer a consistent Monday–Friday schedule aligned with standard business hours (approximately 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM), as this allows me to maintain reliable transportation and family responsibilities. I do not currently have childcare obligations, but I do help my parents with medical appointments roughly once a month; when possible, I try to schedule those early morning or late afternoon with advance notice.

I am open to occasional overtime, especially during busy HR periods such as open enrollment, year-end reporting, or large onboarding waves, as long as I have at least 24–48 hours’ notice when possible. I also understand that HR sometimes needs to be flexible for urgent employee relations issues or time-sensitive compliance items and am willing to adjust my schedule when those situations arise.

I rely on my own car for commuting and do not have any regular public transportation constraints. I do, however, have a weekly community volunteer commitment on Sunday afternoons and would prefer to keep Sundays fully off if at all possible. If there is an emergency HR situation that requires remote support on a weekend, I’m willing to log in from home as needed.

I do not have any medical restrictions that affect my working hours, and I am comfortable attending occasional early-morning or after-hours meetings with managers or leadership as part of the HR role.
